graphEvents.label: safeGetDb warn-once flag masks distinct DB-failure causes
Zero-fallback catch in memory_health swallows error classification
Env-flag tests miss numeric and whitespace edge cases
Re-validated: still open (save commit hook test not end-to-end)

title: safeGetDb warn-once flag masks distinct DB-failure causes
Zero-fallback catch in memory_health swallows error classification
Env-flag tests miss numeric and whitespace edge cases

detail: All 3 new findings are P2 (non-blocking). No P0/P1 discovered. Two observability refinements, one test-coverage gap.

direction: memory-save cache wiring after failed commit
reason: writeTransaction.immediate() at line 2532 either commits and returns ID or throws. ReconsolidationAbort returns early; other errors propagate. Cache invalidation at line 2583 is only reached after successful commit. Post-insert enrichment at lines 2577-2582 wraps all 5 steps in try-catch via runEnrichmentStep (post-insert.ts:244-251), so enrichment failure returns EnrichmentStepResult rather than throwing.
Line 149 (early return) and line 256 (post-transaction) are mutually exclusive: line 149 fires when affectedCount===0 with immediate return; lines 222-256 run only when affectedCount>0.

All routing reasons are hardcoded ASCII internal strings (e.g., graph-preserved-by-intent=24 chars). MAX_ROUTING_REASON_LENGTH=120 is far above any current reason. clampRoutingReason at line 298-301 is future-proofing, not a current risk.
test-env.ts:35-37 attaches .finally() to Promise return values, which fires on both fulfillment and rejection. Sync exceptions (line 40-43) go through the catch block. vitest uses standard Promises.
vitest workers (threads/forks) have isolated process.env copies. Within-worker, vitest runs describe-block tests sequentially. setEnv/restoreEnv pattern is correct for this model.
Implementation at query-router.ts:186 uses raw.trim().toLowerCase() which correctly normalizes ' 0 ', ' FALSE ', etc. to known values. The code handles whitespace; the tests just don't cover it.
